item.title: Iran, Israel voice caveats on deal ahead of expected signing ceremony
item.description: Senior U.S. officials say President Trump and Iran's top negotiator have already remotely signed a memorandum of understanding ahead of an expected signing ceremony.

item.title: Iran World Cup team coach says it was booted from U.S. right after first match
item.description: Iran's World Cup team coach says it was ordered to leave the U.S. and return to its training base in Mexico only a few hours after opening its politically charged tournament with a draw.

item.title: U.S. citizen arrested over alleged child sexual abuse in Colombia
item.description: Three children were found by authorities when they entered the suspect's apartment and were taken to a medical center to be evaluated, officials said.

item.title: Humpback whale caught in fishing rope freed by responders
item.description: A young humpback whale was freed from entanglement in fishing gear off the coast of Massachusetts on Sunday.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: American doctor who recovered from Ebola back in U.S., says he's "feeling well"
item.description: Dr. Peter Stafford, his wife, Rebekah Stafford, and their four children all arrived safely on Monday, according to Serge, a Pennsylvania-based Christian missions organization.

item.title: 6/15: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Details still slim on U.S.-Iran deal; California Gov. Gavin Newsom says the Justice Department is investigating him and his wife.

item.title: 8 dead in B-52 bomber crash at Edwards Air Force Base in California, officials say
item.description: The aircraft was on a routine test mission at Edwards airfield, located in the western Mojave Desert, about 100 miles northeast of Los Angeles.

item.title: Middle East expert on U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: An initial agreement between the U.S. and Iran to extend their shaky ceasefire is nearing formal signing. Mona Yacoubian, director of the Middle East program at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, joins with analysis.

item.title: Teen accused of killing stepsister on cruise to be jailed pending trial
item.description: The teen was initially released pending trial after being charged as a juvenile, but after he was charged as an adult, a judge ruled he was no longer subject to rules regarding juvenile detention.

item.title: Parts of Texas bracing for more rain after flooding
item.description: Thunderstorms in parts of central Texas caused severe flooding over the weekend, and more rain is on the way. CBS News correspondent Jason Allen has the latest.

item.title: Trump makes last minute endorsement in Georgia GOP Senate runoff
item.description: After months of abstaining from the Georgia Republican Senate primary race, President Trump has endorsed Mike Collins ahead of Tuesday's runoff. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry reports from Atlanta.

item.title: Data shows Trump's investment accounts traded up to $695 million in stocks over 3 months
item.description: New data from the Office of Government Ethics shows that President Trump is ramping up his stock portfolio, with a CBS News analysis finding the president's investment accounts made 3,600 trades between January and March. CBS News senior investigative reporter Michael Kaplan has more.

item.title: Newsom says he's under investigation by Justice Department
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom is accusing the Justice Department of abusing the grand jury process after he says it launched a federal investigation into him and his wife. CBS News California investigative correspondent Julie Watts has the details.

item.title: Protests planned in California as Iran prepares for Men's World Cup match against New Zealand
item.description: Some protests are expected in Southern California on Monday night as Iran's soccer team prepares to face New Zealand in its first match of the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes reports from Los Angeles.

item.title: What has the U.S. accomplished with the Iran war?
item.description: President Trump is touting a preliminary agreement with Iran that would kick off 60 days of talks to negotiate a final deal. Ian Bremmer, founder and president of the Eurasia Group, joins with analysis.

item.title: Early details on preliminary U.S.-Iran deal to end war
item.description: President Trump is touting an electronically signed preliminary deal with Iran to end the war, but the full text has yet to be released. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Imtiaz Tyab report.

item.title: Edwards Air Force Base official says B-52 jet crashed immediately after takeoff, deemed unsurvivable
item.description: Officials with Edwards Air Force Base held a news conference on Monday after a B-52 Stratofortress jet carrying eight people crashed in Kern County, California. Officials said the crash was deemed unsurvivable.

item.title: Japanese fans' tradition of cleaning up after themselves at World Cup
item.description: Japanese soccer fans at the World Cup are continuing a tradition they started in 1998: cleaning up their own trash at stadiums. Tony Dokoupil reports.

item.title: Cellphone fire on flight "scorched" cabin before landing in Las Vegas
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said British Airways Flight 271 landed safely at Harry Reid International Airport in Las Vegas after the crew reported a cellphone fire on board.

item.title: Iran makes its debut at 2026 World Cup amid war, visa restrictions
item.description: Iran will kick off its first match of the 2026 World Cup on Monday night against New Zealand. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for kids under 16
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a proposed law Monday that would ban anyone under 16 from social media apps like TikTok and Snapchat, and the social media companies themselves would be required to verify ages or face big fines. Leigh Kiniry has details from London.

item.title: Newsom says Justice Department is investigating him and his wife
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom, a Democrat, said the Justice Department is investigating he and his wife, Jennifer.

item.title: 6/15: CBS Evening News
item.description: 8 believed dead in B-52 crash at California's Edwards Air Force Base; Gov. Gavin Newsom says he's under federal investigation.

item.title: The role of debate in America's founding
item.description: As the U.S. prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, a new book is examining the foundation the Founding Fathers laid for generations to come. Tony Williams and David Bobb, authors of "Divided Over the Declaration," join to discuss.

item.title: California daycare remains open after children test positive for drugs, investigation finds
item.description: About half a dozen parents told CBS California Investigates they had not been informed of the daycare's probationary status, even though the state requires the facility to inform all current and prospective parents.

item.title: Vance says Trump is "very supportive" but doesn't confirm 2028 presidential run
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance spoke with "CBS Sunday Morning" about his new book, their growing family, and whether he's considering a 2028 presidential run. CBS News' Robert Costa has more.

item.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for children under 16
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer hopes to have a ban on kids using social media that is "designed to be addictive" enacted by early next year.

item.title: Referee denied entry into U.S. was talking to "very bad people," official says
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the executive director of the White House's World Cup task force and the son of former NYC Mayor Rudy Giuliani, said he stood by the U.S.'s decision to reject Omar Artan.

item.title: They're uninsured after Obamacare became too costly. They're far from alone.
item.description: After congressional Republicans let expanded subsidies for Affordable Care Act plans expire at the end of last year, some families have decided the price is too great of a financial burden and canceled their coverage.
